title: Bilinear Limb-Kernel Splits: Minimizing Scalar Multiplications for a Fixed Coefficient Tensor
strategies: trivial: schoolbook split -- form every product x_i*y_j (R = p*q), reproduces the checker baseline (~0.1)
greedy: support-pruned schoolbook -- skip products whose coefficient column T[:,i,j] is all-zero; beats baseline on sparse tensors but ignores linear-rank structure (middling)

strong: mode-wise rank exploitation -- rank-1 peel the slices of the best tensor mode (output / x / y), emitting the smallest valid split; captures the planted low linear rank, far fewer products, but only an upper bound on the unknown true bilinear rank (headroom remains)
invalid: emit an all-zero (or otherwise non-reconstructing) split -> exact identity fails -> 0
